expectations.text: Uses the e2e_judge_orchestrator.py as the entry point
expectations.evidence: Command section shows: 'python -m tests.e2e_judge_orchestrator' as the exact entry point
Command shows '--journeys login,home,chat' and explanation says 'Limits the run to only the three requested journeys'

expectations.text: Passes the ALB URL via --base-url flag
Passes auth credentials via --auth-email and --auth-password flags
expectations.evidence: Command shows '--auth-email testuser@example.com --auth-password "EagleTest2024!"' with explanation of why the password is quoted

expectations.text: Mentions expected output: results JSON, markdown report, and screenshots directory
expectations.evidence: Section '4. Expected Output > File outputs' table lists: 'results/{run-id}.json', 'results/{run-id}-report.md', and 'screenshots/{run-id}/login/*.png' etc.

expectations.text: Includes pre-flight checks (AWS creds, playwright installed, URL reachable)
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Pre-flight Checks' covers all three: (a) pip install playwright check, (b) aws sts get-caller-identity for creds, (c) curl ALB URL for reachability, plus (d) ls orchestrator file

claims.claim: Chat journey waits 90s for agent response
claims.evidence: Consistent with e2e_judge_journeys.py chat journey timeout of 90_000ms

suggestions.reason: This assertion is well-targeted — it checks that exactly the requested journeys are passed, not more, not fewer. Could be even stronger by verifying the response explicitly explains WHY other journeys are excluded.

eval_feedback.overall: Solid assertions for an explicit-prompt eval. All are discriminating — each would fail if the skill didn't produce the orchestrator command correctly.
